---

## Why It Works

I take the "enemy" (productivity gurus) and take a stand against them. Then I flip their narrative and show that with my contrarian take, I've built an even better, more life-first business.

## Template

I never do [POPULAR TREND].
I don't [POPULAR TREND].
I don't always start my day with [POPULAR TREND].
I forget to [POPULAR TREND].
I might go 6 months without [POPULAR TREND].
I'm not interested in [POPULAR TREND].
Not everything is [POPULAR TREND], [POPULAR TREND], or [POPULAR TREND].
Sometimes a guy just wants to [CONTRARIAN TAKE].
[CONTRARIAN TAKE].
[CONTRARIAN TAKE].
Hell, maybe even [CONTRARIAN TAKE].
I'm not a [DESCRIPTION OF PEOPLE WHO FOLLOW POPULAR TRENDS].
So I don't try to be one.
But, I've [ACCOMPLISHED BIG GOAL BY DOING CONTRARIAN THINGS ABOVE].
What about you?
What's your best advice for [RELEVANT PEOPLE]?

## Example

Here's an example using the template, written from the perspective of an SAT Tutor:

I never do flashcards.
I don't memorize formulas.
I don't always start my day with practice tests.
I forget to time my sections.
I might go 6 months without looking at an SAT prep book.
I'm not interested in speed-reading techniques.
Not everything is drills, worksheets, or vocabulary lists.
Sometimes a guy just wants to teach students how to think.
Actually understand the why behind math problems.
Learn to read critically instead of scanning for answers.
Hell, maybe even enjoy the process of learning.
I'm not a cookie-cutter test prep instructor.
So I don't try to be one.
But, I've helped over 500 students improve their scores by 200+ points.
What about you?
What's your best advice for students drowning in test prep materials?
